title: Re-implement Axis Health and Stagnation Monitoring
problem: Several core belief axes show high confidence (95%) but very low velocity (minimal daily change), suggesting potential belief stagnation. A previous attempt to build a tool to monitor this, 'Build a Belief Stagnation Detector and Axis Health Report', failed due to a technical implementation error, leaving this critical monitoring gap open.
evidence: Belief report data from 2026-04-07 to 2026-04-09 shows minimal score changes. For example, `axis_epistemic_integrity` moved from 0.285 to 0.293, and `axis_power_accountability` fluctuated between -0.467 and -0.451, all while confidence remained at 95%.

The explicit failure of the previous proposal: '[failed] "Build a Belief Stagnation Detector and Axis Health Report" — Pipeline error: Tests failed: Error: Cannot find module '../../../lib/belief_system''
proposed_solution: Create a new tool, `axis_health_monitor.js`, to be run during the daily maintenance pipeline. This tool will read `state/belief_history.jsonl` to analyze the recent history of all high-confidence axes. For each axis, it will calculate metrics such as '7-day velocity' (rate of change) and '30-day volatility' (standard deviation). The tool will generate an 'Axis Health' section in the daily report, flagging axes as 'stagnant' if their velocity is below a certain threshold while confidence is high. This re-implementation must include robust tests to prevent the previous module import failure.
